mnemonics.title: Input/Output Statements
mnemonics.content: Imagine a conversation between a user and a computer. The user asks a question (input), and the computer responds with an answer (output).

mnemonics.title: File Input/Output
mnemonics.content: Think of a file as a book. To read from a file, you open the book (open() function) and read the contents (read() method). To write to a file, you open the book (open() function) and write new content (write() method).

acronyms.usage: I/O statements are used to read input from the user and display output to the user.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a file as a physical folder. To read from a file, you open the folder and read the contents. To write to a file, you open the folder and add new content.

analogies.analogy: A conversation between a user and a computer is like a conversation between two people. The user asks a question (input), and the computer responds with an answer (output).
